name: erdos_szemeredi_sum_product
claim_latex: Erdős–Szemerédi sum-product conjecture: for every $\varepsilon > 0$ there is a constant $c_\varepsilon > 0$ such that for every finite $A \subset \mathbb{Z}_{>0}$ with $|A| \geq 2$, $\max(|A + A|, |A \cdot A|) \geq c_\varepsilon |A|^{2 - \varepsilon}$. Operationally, this verifier checks the strictly-stronger combined form: $|A + A| + |A \cdot A| \geq |A|^{2 - \varepsilon}$ for the value of $\varepsilon$ in the witness payload (default $\varepsilon = 2/3$, Solymosi regime).

given_facts.statement: Solymosi (2009): for every finite $A \subset \mathbb{R}_{>0}$ with $|A| \geq 2$, $\max(|A+A|, |A \cdot A|) \gg \frac{|A|^{4/3}}{(\log |A|)^{1/3}}$ unconditionally.
given_facts.sign_disambiguation: LOWER bound on $\max(|A+A|, |A \cdot A|)$. The exponent $4/3$ corresponds to $\varepsilon = 2/3$ (modulo the $\log^{1/3}$ factor, which only weakens the bound by a logarithmic factor for finite $A$). Reading F1 as an UPPER bound on the max is a sign error. F1 is unconditional; F2 sharpens it.
warns: F1 establishes the 4/3 bound. A witness violating $\varepsilon = 2/3$ at any specific $|A|$ would refute F1, which is a published theorem — so a 'witness' here is actually a check that proposed proofs do not contradict F1.

given_facts.statement: Konyagin–Shkredov / Rudnev–Stevens refinement (2020): for every finite $A \subset \mathbb{R}_{>0}$ with $|A| \geq 2$, $\max(|A+A|, |A \cdot A|) \geq c |A|^{4/3 + 2/1167 - o(1)}$ for an explicit absolute constant $c > 0$.
given_facts.sign_disambiguation: LOWER bound, sharper than F1 by an exponent of $2/1167 \approx 0.0017$. This is the strongest known unconditional bound; beating it (i.e. proving a sharper exponent) would be a research-frontier result. Reading the exponent as an upper bound or treating $2/1167$ as negligible (and rounding the exponent down to $4/3$) loses the refinement — flag as a sign-attentiveness lapse.
warns: F2 says the exponent is at least $4/3 + 2/1167$, not just $4/3$. A proof attempt that targets exponent $\geq 4/3 + 2/1167$ matches the literature; targeting strictly more (e.g. $\geq 4/3 + 1/100$) is open and a meaningful partial result.

given_facts.statement: Erdős–Szemerédi 1983 (original conjecture): for every $\varepsilon > 0$ there exists $c_\varepsilon > 0$ such that $\max(|A+A|, |A \cdot A|) \geq c_\varepsilon |A|^{2 - \varepsilon}$ for all finite $A \subset \mathbb{Z}_{>0}$.
given_facts.sign_disambiguation: ASYMPTOTIC quantifier-over-$\varepsilon$. The conjecture states that the exponent can be pushed arbitrarily close to $2$, but it is only a quantifier-over-$\varepsilon$ statement: for each $\varepsilon > 0$ separately. Concluding that $\max \geq |A|^{2}$ unconditionally (the $\varepsilon = 0$ case) is a sign error — the conjecture does NOT assert this; only the limit $\varepsilon \to 0^+$ approaches it.
warns: F3 is the open conjecture itself; F1 and F2 are the strongest unconditional partial results. A witness against F3 at some specific $\varepsilon$ would refute F3 for that $\varepsilon$, but witnesses for $\varepsilon < 1 - 4/3 \cdot |A|^{-c}$ (i.e. inside F2's proven regime) would also refute F2 and are therefore implausible without a coding bug.

witness_schema.eps: float in (0, 1) — the targeted exponent gap; default 2/3 (Solymosi regime). The verifier checks |A+A|+|A*A| < |A|^(2-eps) strictly.
witness_schema.claimed_combined_cardinality: int (optional) — agent's claim of |A+A|+|A*A|; verifier recomputes.

note: Erdős–Szemerédi sum-product conjecture (1983). Witness contract: a finite distinct-positive-integer set $A$ together with a parameter $\varepsilon \in (0,1)$ such that $|A+A| + |A \cdot A| < |A|^{2-\varepsilon}$. The verifier computes both sumset and productset cardinalities (O(|A|^2) work, capped at |A| <= 5000) and checks the strict inequality against the ceiling of the real-valued threshold. The default $\varepsilon = 2/3$ corresponds to the Solymosi $4/3$-bound regime — beating it for a finite witness would refute Solymosi/Rudnev–Stevens and almost certainly indicate a coding error. The genuinely-open case is $\varepsilon$ slightly larger than $1 - (4/3 + 2/1167)/2 = 1/3 - 1/1167$, where current literature is unable to derive the bound. Track 1 search loop also fits — hunt for sets with anomalously small combined sumset+productset, e.g. variants of the Erdős–Szemerédi multiplicative-additive interpolation. The witness_threshold field is set to 0 because the actual threshold is computed dynamically from |A| and eps inside the verifier; the keep rule fires on is_valid alone.
